transcript.text: Hey, Angela, good afternoon.
This meeting is being recorded.
You're on mute right now.
Always, of course.
That's like the never-ending working remote joke, right?
Like you're talking and you're on mute.
I feel like you haven't truly used Zoom if you haven't been talking on mute before.
It's the rite of passage.
It really is.
I know.
And I've kind of flip-flopped between computers, doing some different things all day.
so, yeah.
How are you?
Where are you located?
Yeah, I'm located in Houston, Texas.
I joined GrowthX three weeks ago as their first account executive slash sales hire.
And week two, they sent me out to San Francisco.
So it was great.
That's amazing.
Wow.
So you're brand new.
you.
you.
So the team, good for you.
I've been the first sales hire at Series A before, so I'm used to drinking from the fire hose, but this is definitely the most I've ever done in three weeks.

And I will say, despite being new, I will be able to make good use of your time, and you'll be able to, you know, walk away from this meeting with a good understanding of what we do and if we might be the right fit.
Sorry for getting your email confused with Matthew, by the way.
I'm not sure if you saw my email I sent when I changed the Zoom, but I should have seen that.
There's the A before Korish.
No Is Adam joining or just us today?
He is, yep.
I just sent him the Zoom link because knowing him, he's back-to-back as well today, so I just sent him the Zoom link, so he should be here any second.
Perfect.
Awesome.
Thank you.
you.
Fun fact here, I'm actually going to Oregon for the first time in a week and a half for my bachelor party, and Bend is one of the places that we're going to go.
No way.
Oh, cool.
Well, sweet.
Well, you should let me know when you're here, and depending what your extra time looks like, you can come check out our Patiofire facility.
That's actually where I am right now.
Super cool.
The guys are cranking out, cranking out heaters.
Yeah, we're, we're a brand new startup.
We're about a year in.
We're based in Bend, Oregon, which you saw.
And hey, Adam.
And we make premium wood pellet heaters.
They're warmer than anything on the market.
They're sexier than anything on the market.
They're made of heavy gauge steel.
They're about 70 pounds, so they don't blow over in the wind like all the other heaters.
And we're super excited.
So they're selling like wildfire.
And yeah.
We're about a year in, and we're excited to grow this beast.
And here's Adam.
Adam, I'll let you introduce yourself.
You look like you're in a closet.
I'm Adam.
I'm leading marketing at Patio Fire.
I've also got experience in the agency business.
And, yeah, you know, we're kind of trying to build an AI-first marketing.
We have been building an AI-first marketing function, you know, and trying to build that, you know, marketing department of one, which I think we're doing pretty good.
There's two of us.
Well, there's less than a single FTE equivalent on the team, right?
Because everyone's kind of, you know, has multiple things going on.
But, yeah, I mean, we just got a new CEO.
We're looking for funding.
So we want to scale and really want to understand what's
Out there and how you guys are working.
Yeah, absolutely, Adam.
Just to introduce myself quickly, Angela and I were chatting, but I'm the first sales hire here at GrowthX.
It's week three for me.
I'm based in Houston, Texas, and I'll be traveling to Oregon for the first time in a week and a half for my bachelor party.
So I'm really excited about that.
I guess my first question for you here is like, where are you guys at in the AI marketing journey?
Like how far along would you say you are?
What's the biggest hurdle you're trying to overcome?
I'm sure that's why we're talking to that.
Yeah, I mean, I don't think we've gotten very far with AI agents, you know, the workflow automation and agents are kind of, I think, really where the unlock will be.
We do, you know, all our communications planning is using ChatGBT5, you know, we're...
Using, like, Claude and ChatGPT for content creation.
So it's allowed us to do a lot quickly, you know, trying to do SEO blog posts with that.
And, you know, we kind of start concepting with AI, you know, when we're doing creative.
We do have a designer on the team.
And we're not doing, I don't know if we're doing the volume yet of ad units to make AI banners worthwhile, you know.
But we're definitely looking to, like, how do we drive track and build a successful e-commerce brand?
Yeah, for sure.
And I guess when you think about where GrowthX would fit in to that journey, like, what we would support with.
Think of us as an end-to-end partner from a perspective of creating content that drives organic growth.
Right?
Drives traffic to your website.
So we'll do everything from the strategy to identifying the opportunities where you guys, right, could go after certain keywords or, you know, go after certain topics.
And then our bread and butter is really what you've mentioned, right?
More blog content, more editorial.
We, sorry.
It's like a, it's like a huge echo.
Oh, is that me?
Let me see if I'm, I'm in a booth.
You just turn the fan on?
I don't, I don't know.
It it was weird.
I could like hear myself echo back in like a super distracting way, but it's, it's all good.
I'll let you know if it comes back.
Um, but yeah, we, we typically work with more like B2B software companies.
To tell you the truth.
And, um, I guess I was curious, like what your main interest in growth X like was because we would help you.
You guys, from a content marketing perspective, but we wouldn't do anything with paid ads.
And I guess I just wanted to better understand that before I dove too deep today.
And sorry, you'll probably have to come off mute.
Someone had mentioned you guys to us.
And, you know, we're trying to figure out with search engines declining, like, how do you actually get organic traffic today?
Like, is it possible?
You know, like I'm reading about companies losing 85% of their search traffic.
Yeah, we're not B2B, we're more e-commerce focused.
But, you know, we're, you know, I don't know if that is viable for an e-commerce brand, but, you know, love your opinion on that.
Yeah, definitely.
And I think I would, you know, chat with the team to figure out if we're, you know, the right fit for what you're looking for.
But I guess you came into this conversation today, like looking for a partner that could help you guys scale content and show up more in LLM search, or were you looking for something different?

I think we were looking, we saw that as, we see that as kind of part of the pie, you know, but like how do we kind of scale, go from writing copy and pasting it into an email to having all our email, you know, kind of dynamically generated and published on, you know, with some checks along the way.
Understood.
So website concept, but also like email marketing.
What other services are you looking for in a partner?
Are those the main two?
And is there anything else that you're thinking of?
I don't think it's going to be effective.
Cool for like banner ads.
I saw on your site you do Facebook buys or LinkedIn buys or something like that.
I see the most opportunity with integrating with Klaviyo for email SMS and Shopify for web content.
Got it.
If there's an ad opportunity, know, happy to explore that, course.
Yeah, think I can give you all a quick overview, go through some examples, show you how our AI workflows create content.
But I think high level from everything you've told me doesn't seem like we're the best fit.
And I agree there's some stuff on our website that needs to be updated to better clarify the lanes that we operate in and what we do.
But high level, right, like growthx, we are an agency partner, but we have a lot of
Workflows and an internal platform with experts in the loop to help you create content at scale, right?

And the reason we're doing that is because in this transition from SEO to GEO or this evolution where you have to think about GEO and how you show up and chat GPT and other tools, clicks are disappearing.
But the visitors that hit your site are much more high intent, right?
They're like 4.4 times more likely to convert.
They spend three times as long.
And so the question is, how do you show up when there is such a long tail of possible ways someone could go in and interact with the chat GPT?
And what we think the solution is, is to create content at scale, right?
And to keep the content fresh.
And so that's why what we do, which is creating editorial content, refresh.
Fishing content, maybe thinking about programmatic SEO plays.
You know, that's why we think it's valuable is because we're able to help you show up where it matters most.
So, you know, the- What is this chart measuring?
What are the 95, 92?
What are these?
This is just like the most important factors from like a GEO or AEO perspective in terms of like what LLMs value.
Okay.
Yeah.
And so, you know, today, like a lot of our brands, as I mentioned, they're technology companies.
And, you know, what the way that we're engaging is there's two phases.
There's an eight-week strategy sprint where we would create these artifacts because the content we create is only as good as the context that we feed into it.
So it's information about the personas you go after, right?
It's information about like their jobs to be done.
And then, obviously, this is a little bit, yeah.
Different than what you would be thinking about because it's like people are buying a product from you, right?
They're not like buying a service.
And then in the growth execution phase, you know, that's where we're like creating content over a 12-month period.
So, you know, when you think about the kind of content that we make, Tiro was a very small company that came to us.
You know, we do a lot of their blog content.
But this is content that we create for them.
We work with companies like Ramp, where we did their vendor catalog.
You can see that they've got like proprietary data that feeds into the information and all of these different pieces.

And that's really what we're doing, Adam and Angela, is, you know, we're creating content that's going to help them show up where people might be having conversations and, right, trying to learn more about solutions or products that you offer.
I can continue to kind of go deeper, but honestly, I'm just...
I'm sure it's the best fit, and I'm happy to defer to you, yeah.
There's a lot in common with B2B and considered purchases, but I don't know if this is the right fit for us either.
I mean, we're certainly trying to create blog content that, you know, around patio design, patio furniture, you know, what kind of heaters are the best, like, and do authoritative content in that.
Vain.
Right, but you're looking for much more than just, like, a partner that's going to help you guys create blog content at scale, correct?
Yeah.
I mean, when you say at scale, what does scale look like?
At scale would look like daily publishing for clients.
We do 10 articles per week as part of, like, our base package.
So, you know, augment codes.
A good example of a client that came to us with about 5,000 per month in organic, and then after about six months with growthx, know, they're 5 to 10xing their traffic.
The way that, you know, the content's created, just to, like, pull back the curtain a little bit more, we did all these guides for them on their site.
And for this piece in particular here, 13 Best AI Coding Tools for Enterprise Codebases, like, even this chart was created through our workflow.
And so, like, what the workflow looks like would be, you've got, you know, these pipelines where we're creating content, and one is, like, this article generation workflow.
Another might be an article with an image or a comparison article.
And so, for that specific piece, what's happening is, you know, we are creating a brief where, for the...
Content that's going to be created.

We're going and doing deep research, creating an outline for the article, draft, fact-checking against third-party sources, cross-linking to other content that they have on their site, and then generating the SEO details.

But what's interesting about this piece here is if I inspect any of these different workflows, you'll see that there are hundreds of steps that we have automated so that it's not just creating AI-generated content with no context behind it.
It's using the artifacts about the company, the personas, and then it's putting that all through this workflow to where it can fetch keyword traffic.
It can grab sort of ranking factors, analyze competitor content, and then use all that information to inform the brief.
If you continue into, like, the research step, we're generating research questions.
We're performing deep research on each of those questions.

And then we're even like, you know, fact checking the article, the content that's created, passage by passage, and then suggesting rewrites if there's, you know, a confidence interval that isn't high enough, and we can't like verify that it's accurate, right?
So it goes back to like, what are LLMs rewarding?
You know, they're rewarding content that's well-researched, comprehensive, and growthx.
our differentiator is, you know, the person who founded this company, he was an early content marketing leader at IBM and HP.
He was the CMO of companies that scaled and had successful assets.
And he did this exact same experiment at DeepGround, where they 24x their traffic and 24x their revenue in a year.
And then he built out a team around this with, you know, folks who were head of SEO at TripAdvisor.
Manager in Fanatics, managing editor at GrowthX who built the G2 website.
And so, you know, what we would do is like we'd help you guys drive traffic through the editorial content on your site, but we wouldn't go beyond that.
And so that's the big thing to consider when it comes to GrowthX.
Got it.
You wouldn't need like a, you know, SEO agency and then a creative agency, or you wouldn't need to hire freelancers to write the content and create it at scale.
We would do everything for you.
It's just in a specific lane of marketing as opposed to advertising, email, and all the other things that you mentioned.
Got it.
This has been really helpful.
I'm going to be respectful of your time, let you go, but thank you.
If you guys, you know, do think that there's something for like a considered purchase like this, let me know.
Let us know.
But otherwise, I really appreciate
I your time and yeah, if I, we'll definitely, yeah, there's other things I might be working on that could be a fit, so.
100%, and Adam, guess who sent you our way just to like better understand that?
I'm trying to remember who that was.
I was at like this AI networking event in San Francisco, this meetup thing, and someone mentioned it, but I don't remember who it was.

Yeah, I don't think, I don't think we have any e-commerce clients today, but I will dig in and see if like we have one and if that is a vertical we're planning to do more in, but I'm glad I could give you a quick overview and I'll let you get back to it.
Yeah, it was nice to meet you.
Take care.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce GrowthX's AI-driven content marketing services to Patiofyre, a startup producing premium wood pellet heaters.](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX specializes in AI-driven content creation for organic growth, focusing on B2B software companies](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
  - [Patiofyre, an e-commerce startup, seeks broader marketing support beyond just content creation](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=479.0)
  - [While there's mutual interest, GrowthX's current offerings don't fully align with Patiofyre's diverse marketing needs](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)
  - [Both parties agreed that GrowthX may not be the best fit for Patiofyre's current requirements](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)

## Topics

### Patiofyre's Business Overview

  - [Founded \~1 year ago, based in Bend, Oregon](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=147.0)
  - [Produces premium wood pellet heaters (70 lbs, heavy-gauge steel)](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=156.0)
  - [Products are "warmer and sexier" than competitors, selling rapidly](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=161.0)
  - [Recently appointed new CEO, currently seeking funding to scale](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)

### Patiofyre's Current AI Marketing Efforts

  - [Using ChatGPT-5 for communications planning](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=290.0)
  - [Leveraging Claude and ChatGPT for content creation, including SEO blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [AI-assisted creative concepting](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=315.0)
  - [Not yet utilizing AI for ad banner creation due to low volume](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=327.0)

### GrowthX's Services and Capabilities

  - [End-to-end partner for organic growth-driven content](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=352.0)
  - [Specialize in blog content and editorial pieces](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=375.0)
  - [Use AI workflows to create content at scale (10 articles/week base package)](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [8-week strategy sprint followed by 12-month growth execution phase](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=702.0)
  - [Success story: Augment Codes 5-10x'd organic traffic in 6 months](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)

### GrowthX's AI Content Creation Process

  - [Utilizes extensive workflows with hundreds of automated steps](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)
  - [Incorporates company artifacts, personas, and jobs-to-be-done](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=710.0)
  - [Performs deep research, fact-checking, and SEO optimization](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=952.0)
  - [Creates comprehensive, well-researched content rewarded by LLMs](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=961.0)

### Misalignment of Needs and Services

  - [Patiofyre seeks broader marketing support (email, SMS, Shopify integration)](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=553.0)
  - [GrowthX focuses primarily on editorial content for organic growth](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=815.0)
  - [GrowthX typically works with B2B software companies, not e-commerce](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)
  - [Patiofyre's considered purchase model shares similarities with B2B, but differences remain](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=785.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Adam to consider GrowthX for potential future projects that align better with their services](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=1070.0)
  - [Tyler to investigate if GrowthX has any e-commerce clients or plans to expand in that vertical](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)
  - [No immediate collaboration planned, but both parties open to future opportunities if better alignment is found](https://fathom.video/share/zreHVo9BkGoskoM7Fy21ux4cV82LfULr?tab=summary&timestamp=1134.0)

action_items.description: Check if GrowthX has e-commerce clients or plans to expand into e-commerce vertical
